results.question: Which equipment requires quarterly inspection?
results.expected: Compressors COMP-C01 require quarterly bearing inspection per OISD-116. Equipment tags EQ-1001 through EQ-4002 undergo quarterly inspections as per safety manual Section 3.
results.got: According to the retrieved context, all tank foundations require quarterly visual inspection. Specifically, Source 1 states: "2.3 All tanks require visual inspection of foundations quarterly." No other equipment (pressure vessels, heat exchangers, pumps) is mentioned as requiring quarterly inspectio

results.question: What is the inspection frequency for pressure vessels TNK-T01 and TNK-T02?
results.expected: Pressure vessels TNK-T01 and TNK-T02 require annual internal inspection per OISD-116. Tank TNK-T03 requires biennial internal inspection under OISD-117 as a Group B tank.
results.got: Pressure vessels TNK-T01 and TNK-T02 must undergo annual internal inspection per OISD-116 guidelines. Source 1 states that all pressure vessels, including storage tanks TNK-T01 through TNK-T03, require annual internal inspection. Source 2 classifies TNK-T01 and TNK-T02 as Group A (hazardous material

results.question: When is a hot work permit required?
results.expected: A hot work permit is mandatory for any work involving open flames, sparks, or temperatures exceeding 30°C above ambient in hazardous areas, per OISD-116 Section 3.1.
results.got: According to OISD-116, hot work permits are mandatory for any work involving open flames, sparks, or temperatures exceeding 30°C above ambient in hazardous areas [Source 1].

results.question: How many emergency response teams are required per shift?
results.expected: Each plant must maintain a minimum of 2 emergency response teams per shift, per OISD-116 Section 4.1.
results.got: According to OISD-116 standards, a minimum of 2 emergency response teams are required per shift [Source 1].

results.question: What are the PPE requirements for mining workers?
results.expected: All workers entering mining areas must wear mandatory PPE including hard hats, safety boots, high-visibility vests, and safety glasses, per DGMS Circular 2023-01. Respiratory protection must be provided for dusty environments, and hearing protection is mandatory in areas exceeding 85 dB.
results.got: According to DGMS Circular 2023-01, all workers entering mining areas must wear mandatory personal protective equipment (PPE) including hard hats, safety boots, high-visibility vests, and safety glasses [Source 1]. The Factory Act Section 41B also requires adequate and suitable PPE for workers in ha

results.question: How often must safety officers be appointed in a factory?
results.expected: Every factory having 1000 or more workers must employ a qualified safety officer per Factory Act Section 7A. The safety officer must hold a degree or diploma in industrial safety.
results.got: According to Factory Act Section 7A, every factory with 1000 or more workers must employ a qualified safety officer. The Act does not specify a recurring appointment frequency; the requirement is based on the factory's workforce size and the officer's qualifications (degree or diploma in industrial

results.question: What is the minimum fresh air supply requirement for underground mines?
results.expected: Fresh air supply must be maintained at a minimum of 0.1 m³/s per worker in underground mines, per DGMS Circular 2022-05.
results.got: The minimum fresh air supply requirement for underground mines is 0.1 m³/s per worker, as specified in DGMS Circular 2022-05 [Source 1].

results.question: How frequently must fire drills be conducted in a factory?
results.expected: Fire drills must be conducted at least once every quarter in factories, per Factory Act Section 36.
results.got: According to the Factory Act Section 36, fire drills must be conducted at least once every quarter (i.e., every three months) in a factory [Source 1].

results.question: What are the requirements for HAZOP studies?
results.expected: HAZOP studies must be conducted for all new installations and modifications to existing units per OISD-118. Revalidation of HAZOP studies must occur every 5 years, and action items from PHA studies must be tracked to completion.
results.got: According to OISD-118, HAZOP studies must be revalidated every 5 years, and action items from Process Hazard Analysis (PHA) studies must be tracked to completion [Source 1].

results.question: What is the inspection frequency for high-pressure piping systems?
results.expected: High-pressure piping (>15 bar) requires annual thickness measurement per OISD-119. Medium-pressure piping (5-15 bar) requires biennial inspection, and low-pressure piping (<5 bar) requires triennial inspection.
results.got: According to OISD-119, high-pressure piping systems (>15 bar) require annual thickness measurement [Source 1].

results.question: How quickly must serious factory accidents be reported?
results.expected: All accidents resulting in death or serious injury must be reported to the factory inspector within 12 hours per Factory Act Section 40A. The investigation must establish root cause and contributing factors.
results.got: According to Factory Act Section 40A, all accidents resulting in death or serious injury must be reported to the factory inspector within 12 hours [Source 1].

results.question: What rights do workers have regarding workplace safety?
results.expected: Workers have the right to be informed about hazards, receive safety training, report unsafe conditions without fear of retaliation, and refuse to work in immediate danger to life or health, per Factory Act Section 41C.
results.got: Under the Factory Act Section 41C, workers have the following rights regarding workplace safety: (a) the right to be informed about hazards at their workplace; (b) the right to receive training on safety procedures; (c) the right to report unsafe conditions without fear of retaliation; and (d) the r

results.question: What must be fenced according to Factory Act Section 38?
results.expected: Every dangerous part of any machinery must be securely fenced, including all moving parts (flywheels, gears, pulleys), parts in motion when machinery is running, and fixed parts which may move to create a danger, per Factory Act Section 38.
results.got:
**[Source 1] FA-SEC-38.txt**

results.question: What safety measures are required for hazardous processes?
results.expected: Factories must provide adequate and suitable PPE, medical examination of workers, safety showers and eye wash stations for hazardous processes per Factory Act Section 41B. Workers must also be trained on hazardous material handling.
results.got: According to Factory Act Section 41B, the following safety measures are required for hazardous processes: (a) provision of adequate and suitable personal protective equipment (PPE) for all workers; (b) medical examination of workers before and during employment; (c) installation of safety showers an

results.question: What are the requirements for gas monitoring in underground mines?
results.expected: Gas monitoring must be conducted at the working face every 2 hours, and air quality must be monitored continuously for methane and carbon monoxide per DGMS Circular 2022-05. Ventilation surveys must be completed weekly.

results.question: How often must safety training be conducted for mining workers?
results.expected: All new workers must complete 40 hours of safety induction training per DGMS Circular 2023-01. Refresher training must be conducted every 6 months, and emergency response drills must be conducted monthly.
results.got: According to DGMS Circular 2023-01, all new workers in mining operations must complete 40 hours of safety induction training, and refresher training is required (though the exact frequency of refresher training is not specified in the provided excerpt) [Source 2].

results.question: What are the electrical safety requirements per OISD-130?
results.expected: All electrical equipment in hazardous areas must be certified for the appropriate zone classification. Switchgear must be inspected annually, grounding systems tested annually, and circuit breakers tested every 6 months per OISD-130.

results.question: How should tank classification affect inspection frequency?
results.expected: Group A tanks (hazardous materials like TNK-T01, TNK-T02) require annual internal inspection and biennial external inspection per OISD-117. Group B tanks (flammable liquids like TNK-T03) require biennial internal inspection and annual external inspection.
